verdict.label: real
verdict.rationale: Across the frames the scene reads as normal handheld phone footage of a large smoke plume; smoke structure, lighting, and building/vehicle geometry look consistent with heavy compression/zoom rather than AI synthesis. This visual impression down-weights STYLE’s high score (weak microtexture is expected at this resolution) and SPATIAL’s mid score (no clear face boundary halos/region mismatches visible), aligning more with PHYSICS/TEMPORAL/WATERMARK low-fake assessments.

evidence.detail: Frames 1-50: The smoke/dust column grows and billows continuously (expected turbulent buoyant plume behavior) without teleportation-like jumps; foreground cars and pedestrians maintain ground contact and show plausible frame-to-frame displacement; no collision/interpenetration artifacts are evident in the clear foreground objects (cars, lamp post, building edges).

Frames 1-2 and 5-7 (algorithm flagged motion-coherence/background-coupling issues): visually, the main change is a slight camera reframe/zoom and evolving smoke texture; rigid background elements (building roofline, lamp post) do not show clear warping tied to foreground motion, suggesting the low coherence/coupling scores are likely driven by compression/low-texture smoke regions rather than a physical-law violation.

Algorithm-reported line-curvature anomalies (notably around frames 1-6 and 10-14) are not clearly visible: straight features like the building roof edge, window rows, fence lines, and the streetlamp remain straight and consistent under the apparent camera motion; any minor wobble is consistent with rolling-shutter/camera shake rather than impossible geometry deformation.

Frames 7/17/20/23/26: ELA boundary analysis flagged halos with max halo_score=2.86 and mean boundary_bg_ratio=2.10 (threshold: 1.5). Visual inspection of the high-contrast smoke/sky edges shows mild ringing/haloing consistent with recompression around sharp luminance transitions; no clear cut-paste seam is unambiguous.

Frames 1/4/13/23/33: Patch inconsistency analysis reported max patch_anomaly_score=0.42 (threshold: 0.3). Visually, some localized texture blocks appear within the smoke plumes and along building edges, consistent with macroblock quantization differences rather than a definite inserted region.

Frames 46/52/56/59/91: Local edge coherence shows very high edge_noise_ratio up to 1.00 and soft_edge_ratio up to 0.75 (mean 0.55), suggesting unusually soft/noisy boundaries. Visually, smoke boundaries look smeared and overly soft relative to nearby rigid edges (lamp post/building), which can indicate either heavy compression/resizing or potential local blending.
Looks like handheld phone footage of a building and smoke plume with plausible real-world geometry; no identifiable game/animation studio/brand source → not classifiable as specific traditional CG.
Across sky/smoke/building, fine detail is heavily suppressed; smoke billows have painterly/denoised look rather than granular turbulent texture; edges are soft and uniform across frames.
